architecture.summary: DaSLaM is a modular reasoning system comprising two components: a small (13B) decomposition generator fine-tuned via policy gradient optimization to decompose complex problems into simpler subproblems, and a solver LM (treated as a black box) that answers the decomposed subproblems. The decomposer learns to interact with and guide the solver through a sequence of subproblems, with information flowing from problem → decomposer → subproblems → solver → answers.
trained_components: decomposition generator (13B LM, policy gradient fine-tuning)

biblio.title: Small Language Models Fine-tuned to Coordinate Larger Language Models improve Complex Reasoning

claims.statement: text-davinci-003 (175B) with DaSLaM decomposition produces competitive or better performance compared to GPT-4
DaSLaM decomposition provides significant performance improvement across solver LMs of diverse sizes
Problem decomposition and solution generation are distinct capabilities better addressed in separate modules than by one monolithic LLM
DaSLaM's modular fine-tuning technique is superior to decomposer LLMs based on prompting alone
A 13B decomposition generator trained via policy gradient can render the decomposition method solver-agnostic
Small, fine-tuned decomposition generators can guide larger solver LMs through multi-step reasoning by learning to decompose problems into subproblems requiring fewer reasoning steps
